---
title: "{{Project Title}}"
subtitle: "scRNA-seq portfolio — Marko Živanović"
author: "Marko Živanović, PhD"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    geometry:
      - top=22mm
      - bottom=22mm
      - left=22mm
      - right=22mm
    fontsize: 10pt
    mainfont: "Helvetica"
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: "blue"
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
---

## Background

Briefly describe the biological context: cell system, disease, why this dataset matters, and what question the analysis addresses. **2–4 sentences.**

## Dataset

- **Source:** {{GEO accession / Zenodo / 10X}}
- **Reference:** {{Author et al., Journal Year, DOI}}
- **Samples:** {{N cells, N samples, conditions}}
- **Platform:** {{10X Chromium v3 / Smart-seq2 / etc.}}

## Methods

Compact methods paragraph — pipeline tool versions, QC thresholds, normalization, integration (if any), clustering resolution, annotation strategy. Reproducible to the level a reviewer could re-run.

## Results

### Cellular landscape

![Hero UMAP showing cluster organization](../figures/hero_umap.png){width=80%}

One paragraph interpreting the cellular landscape.

### Marker genes & annotation

![Top markers per cluster](../figures/marker_heatmap.png){width=85%}

One paragraph on how clusters map to known cell types.

### {{Additional analysis section}}

Velocity / trajectory / DE / communication — whichever is the headline of this project.

## Biological interpretation

Short closing paragraph that ties the analysis back to biology — what the results mean, what would be the natural follow-up experiment, and any caveats. **This is what separates a portfolio piece from a tutorial.**

## Reproducibility

- **Code:** github.com/USERNAME/scrnaseq-portfolio/tree/main/NN_project_name
- **Environment:** conda env from `environment.yml`
- **Random seed:** 42
- **Run time:** {{X minutes on a Y-core machine, Z GB RAM}}

## References

1. ...
2. ...
